title: alsine viscida
intro: Alsine viscida, also known as sticky wintergreen or sticky cinquefoil, is a perennial herb belonging to the rose family. It is native to Europe and North America, with its leaves and stems being covered in a sticky, resinous secretion. The medicinal properties of Alsine viscida lie in its high concentration of salicylic acid, a compound similar to aspirin, which has anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving effects. The health benefits of the plant include treating headaches, fever, and rheumatic pain, and it can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture for these purposes.
